782	export const enum NodeFlags {
783	    None               = 0,
784	    Let                = 1 << 0,  // Variable declaration
785	    Const              = 1 << 1,  // Variable declaration
786	    Using              = 1 << 2,  // Variable declaration
787	    AwaitUsing         = Const | Using, // Variable declaration (NOTE: on a single node these flags would otherwise be mutually exclusive)
788	    NestedNamespace    = 1 << 3,  // Namespace declaration
789	    Synthesized        = 1 << 4,  // Node was synthesized during transformation
790	    Namespace          = 1 << 5,  // Namespace declaration
791	    OptionalChain      = 1 << 6,  // Chained MemberExpression rooted to a pseudo-OptionalExpression
792	    ExportContext      = 1 << 7,  // Export context (initialized by binding)
793	    ContainsThis       = 1 << 8,  // Interface contains references to "this"
794	    HasImplicitReturn  = 1 << 9,  // If function implicitly returns on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
795	    HasExplicitReturn  = 1 << 10,  // If function has explicit reachable return on one of codepaths (initialized by binding)
796	    GlobalAugmentation = 1 << 11,  // Set if module declaration is an augmentation for the global scope
797	    HasAsyncFunctions  = 1 << 12, // If the file has async functions (initialized by binding)
798	    DisallowInContext  = 1 << 13, // If node was parsed in a context where 'in-expressions' are not allowed
799	    YieldContext       = 1 << 14, // If node was parsed in the 'yield' context created when parsing a generator
800	    DecoratorContext   = 1 << 15, // If node was parsed as part of a decorator
801	    AwaitContext       = 1 << 16, // If node was parsed in the 'await' context created when parsing an async function
802	    DisallowConditionalTypesContext = 1 << 17, // If node was parsed in a context where conditional types are not allowed
803	    ThisNodeHasError   = 1 << 18, // If the parser encountered an error when parsing the code that created this node
804	    JavaScriptFile     = 1 << 19, // If node was parsed in a JavaScript
805	    ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError = 1 << 20, // If this node or any of its children had an error
806	    HasAggregatedChildData = 1 << 21, // If we've computed data from children and cached it in this node
807	
808	    // These flags will be set when the parser encounters a dynamic import expression or 'import.meta' to avoid
809	    // walking the tree if the flags are not set. However, these flags are just a approximation
810	    // (hence why it's named "PossiblyContainsDynamicImport") because once set, the flags never get cleared.
811	    // During editing, if a dynamic import is removed, incremental parsing will *NOT* clear this flag.
812	    // This means that the tree will always be traversed during module resolution, or when looking for external module indicators.
813	    // However, the removal operation should not occur often and in the case of the
814	    // removal, it is likely that users will add the import anyway.
815	    // The advantage of this approach is its simplicity. For the case of batch compilation,
816	    // we guarantee that users won't have to pay the price of walking the tree if a dynamic import isn't used.
817	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 22,
818	    /** @internal */ PossiblyContainsImportMeta    = 1 << 23,
819	
820	    JSDoc                                          = 1 << 24, // If node was parsed inside jsdoc
821	    /** @internal */ Ambient                       = 1 << 25, // If node was inside an ambient context -- a declaration file, or inside something with the `declare` modifier.
822	    /** @internal */ InWithStatement               = 1 << 26, // If any ancestor of node was the `statement` of a WithStatement (not the `expression`)
823	    JsonFile                                       = 1 << 27, // If node was parsed in a Json
824	    /** @internal */ TypeCached                    = 1 << 28, // If a type was cached for node at any point
825	    /** @internal */ Deprecated                    = 1 << 29, // If has '@deprecated' JSDoc tag
826	    /** @internal */ Unreachable                   = 1 << 30, // If node is unreachable according to the binder
827	
828	    BlockScoped = Let | Const | Using,
829	    Constant = Const | Using,
830	
831	    ReachabilityCheckFlags = HasImplicitReturn | HasExplicitReturn,
832	    ReachabilityAndEmitFlags = ReachabilityCheckFlags | HasAsyncFunctions,
833	
834	    // Parsing context flags
835	    ContextFlags = DisallowInContext | DisallowConditionalTypesContext | YieldContext | DecoratorContext | AwaitContext | JavaScriptFile | InWithStatement | Ambient,
836	
837	    // Exclude these flags when parsing a Type
838	    TypeExcludesFlags = YieldContext | AwaitContext,
839	
840	    // Represents all flags that are potentially set once and
841	    // never cleared on SourceFiles which get re-used in between incremental parses.

935	    Never = 1 << 1,
936	    Sometimes = Always | Never,
937	}

940	export type NodeId = number;
941	
942	export interface Node extends ReadonlyTextRange {
943	    readonly kind: SyntaxKind;
944	    readonly flags: NodeFlags;

949	    /** @internal */ original?: Node; // The original node if this is an updated node.
950	    /** @internal */ emitNode?: EmitNode; // Associated EmitNode (initialized by transforms)

export const enum NodeCheckFlags {
    None                                     = 0,
    TypeChecked                              = 1 << 0,   // Node has been type checked
    LexicalThis                              = 1 << 1,   // Lexical 'this' reference
    CaptureThis                              = 1 << 2,   // Lexical 'this' used in body
    CaptureNewTarget                         = 1 << 3,   // Lexical 'new.target' used in body
    SuperInstance                            = 1 << 4,   // Instance 'super' reference
    SuperStatic                              = 1 << 5,   // Static 'super' reference
    ContextChecked                           = 1 << 6,   // Contextual types have been assigned
    MethodWithSuperPropertyAccessInAsync     = 1 << 7,   // A method that contains a SuperProperty access in an async context.
    MethodWithSuperPropertyAssignmentInAsync = 1 << 8,   // A method that contains a SuperProperty assignment in an async context.
    CaptureArguments                         = 1 << 9,   // Lexical 'arguments' used in body
    EnumValuesComputed                       = 1 << 10,  // Values for enum members have been computed, and any errors have been reported for them.
    LexicalModuleMergesWithClass             = 1 << 11,  // Instantiated lexical module declaration is merged with a previous class declaration.
    LoopWithCapturedBlockScopedBinding       = 1 << 12,  // Loop that contains block scoped variable captured in closure
    ContainsCapturedBlockScopeBinding        = 1 << 13,  // Part of a loop that contains block scoped variable captured in closure
    CapturedBlockScopedBinding               = 1 << 14,  // Block-scoped binding that is captured in some function
    BlockScopedBindingInLoop                 = 1 << 15,  // Block-scoped binding with declaration nested inside iteration statement
    NeedsLoopOutParameter                    = 1 << 16,  // Block scoped binding whose value should be explicitly copied outside of the converted loop
    AssignmentsMarked                        = 1 << 17,  // Parameter assignments have been marked
    ContainsConstructorReference             = 1 << 18,  // Class or class element that contains a binding that references the class constructor.
    ConstructorReference                     = 1 << 29,  // Binding to a class constructor inside of the class's body.
    ContainsClassWithPrivateIdentifiers      = 1 << 20,  // Marked on all block-scoped containers containing a class with private identifiers.
    ContainsSuperPropertyInStaticInitializer = 1 << 21,  // Marked on all block-scoped containers containing a static initializer with 'super.x' or 'super[x]'.
    InCheckIdentifier                        = 1 << 22,
    PartiallyTypeChecked                     = 1 << 23,  // Node has been partially type checked

    /** These flags are LazyNodeCheckFlags and can be calculated lazily by `hasNodeCheckFlag` */
    LazyFlags = SuperInstance
        | SuperStatic
        | MethodWithSuperPropertyAccessInAsync
        | MethodWithSuperPropertyAssignmentInAsync
        | ContainsSuperPropertyInStaticInitializer
        | CaptureArguments
        | ContainsCapturedBlockScopeBinding
        | NeedsLoopOutParameter
        | ContainsConstructorReference
        | ConstructorReference
        | CapturedBlockScopedBinding
        | BlockScopedBindingInLoop
        | LoopWithCapturedBlockScopedBinding,
}

/** @internal */
export interface EvaluatorResult<T extends string | number | undefined = string | number | undefined> {
    value: T;
    isSyntacticallyString: boolean;
    resolvedOtherFiles: boolean;
    hasExternalReferences: boolean;
}

// dprint-ignore
/** @internal */
export interface NodeLinks {
    flags: NodeCheckFlags;              // Set of flags specific to Node
    calculatedFlags: NodeCheckFlags;    // Set of flags which have definitely been calculated already
    resolvedType?: Type;                // Cached type of type node
    resolvedSignature?: Signature;      // Cached signature of signature node or call expression
    resolvedSymbol?: Symbol;            // Cached name resolution result
    effectsSignature?: Signature;       // Signature with possible control flow effects
    enumMemberValue?: EvaluatorResult;  // Constant value of enum member
    isVisible?: boolean;                // Is this node visible
    containsArgumentsReference?: boolean; // Whether a function-like declaration contains an 'arguments' reference
    hasReportedStatementInAmbientContext?: boolean; // Cache boolean if we report statements in ambient context
    jsxFlags: JsxFlags;                 // flags for knowing what kind of element/attributes we're dealing with
    resolvedJsxElementAttributesType?: Type; // resolved element attributes type of a JSX openinglike element
    resolvedJSDocType?: Type;           // Resolved type of a JSDoc type reference
    switchTypes?: Type[];               // Cached array of switch case expression types
    jsxNamespace?: Symbol | false;      // Resolved jsx namespace symbol for this node
    jsxImplicitImportContainer?: Symbol | false; // Resolved module symbol the implicit jsx import of this file should refer to
    jsxFragmentType?: Type;             // Type of the JSX fragment element, set per SourceFile if a jsxFragment is checked in the file
    contextFreeType?: Type;             // Cached context-free type used by the first pass of inference; used when a function's return is partially contextually sensitive
    deferredNodes?: Set<Node>;          // Set of nodes whose checking has been deferred
    capturedBlockScopeBindings?: Symbol[]; // Block-scoped bindings captured beneath this part of an IterationStatement
    outerTypeParameters?: TypeParameter[]; // Outer type parameters of anonymous object type
    isExhaustive?: boolean | 0;         // Is node an exhaustive switch statement (0 indicates in-process resolution)
    skipDirectInference?: true;         // Flag set by the API `getContextualType` call on a node when `Completions` is passed to force the checker to skip making inferences to a node's type
    declarationRequiresScopeChange?: boolean; // Set by `useOuterVariableScopeInParameter` in checker when downlevel emit would change the name resolution scope inside of a parameter.
    serializedTypes?: Map<string, SerializedTypeEntry>; // Collection of types serialized at this location
    decoratorSignature?: Signature;     // Signature for decorator as if invoked by the runtime.
    spreadIndices?: { first: number | undefined, last: number | undefined }; // Indices of first and last spread elements in array literal
    parameterInitializerContainsUndefined?: boolean; // True if this is a parameter declaration whose type annotation contains "undefined".
    fakeScopeForSignatureDeclaration?: "params" | "typeParams"; // If present, this is a fake scope injected into an enclosing declaration chain.
    assertionExpressionType?: Type;     // Cached type of the expression of a type assertion
    potentialThisCollisions?: Node[];
    potentialNewTargetCollisions?: Node[];
    potentialWeakMapSetCollisions?: Node[];
    potentialReflectCollisions?: Node[];
    potentialUnusedRenamedBindingElementsInTypes?: BindingElement[];
    externalHelpersModule?: Symbol;     // Resolved symbol for the external helpers module
    instantiationExpressionTypes?: Map<number, Type>; // Cache of instantiation expression types for the node
    nonExistentPropCheckCache?: Set<string>;
}

/** @internal */
export type TrackedSymbol = [symbol: Symbol, enclosingDeclaration: Node | undefined, meaning: SymbolFlags];
/** @internal */
export interface SerializedTypeEntry {
    node: TypeNode;
    truncating?: boolean;

export const enum TransformFlags {
    None = 0,

    // Facts
    // - Flags used to indicate that a node or subtree contains syntax that requires transformation.
    ContainsTypeScript = 1 << 0,
    ContainsJsx = 1 << 1,
    ContainsESNext = 1 << 2,
    ContainsES2022 = 1 << 3,
    ContainsES2021 = 1 << 4,
    ContainsES2020 = 1 << 5,
    ContainsES2019 = 1 << 6,
    ContainsES2018 = 1 << 7,
    ContainsES2017 = 1 << 8,
    ContainsES2016 = 1 << 9,
    ContainsES2015 = 1 << 10,
    ContainsGenerator = 1 << 11,
    ContainsDestructuringAssignment = 1 << 12,

    // Markers
    // - Flags used to indicate that a subtree contains a specific transformation.
    ContainsTypeScriptClassSyntax = 1 << 13, // Property Initializers, Parameter Property Initializers
    ContainsLexicalThis = 1 << 14,
    ContainsRestOrSpread = 1 << 15,
    ContainsObjectRestOrSpread = 1 << 16,
    ContainsComputedPropertyName = 1 << 17,
    ContainsBlockScopedBinding = 1 << 18,
    ContainsBindingPattern = 1 << 19,
    ContainsYield = 1 << 20,
    ContainsAwait = 1 << 21,
    ContainsHoistedDeclarationOrCompletion = 1 << 22,
    ContainsDynamicImport = 1 << 23,
    ContainsClassFields = 1 << 24,
    ContainsDecorators = 1 << 25,
    ContainsPossibleTopLevelAwait = 1 << 26,
    ContainsLexicalSuper = 1 << 27,
    ContainsUpdateExpressionForIdentifier = 1 << 28,
    ContainsPrivateIdentifierInExpression = 1 << 29,

    HasComputedFlags = 1 << 31, // Transform flags have been computed.

    // Assertions

    AssertTypeScript = ContainsTypeScript,
    AssertJsx = ContainsJsx,
    AssertESNext = ContainsESNext,
    AssertES2022 = ContainsES2022,
    AssertES2021 = ContainsES2021,
    AssertES2020 = ContainsES2020,
    AssertES2019 = ContainsES2019,
    AssertES2018 = ContainsES2018,
    AssertES2017 = ContainsES2017,
    AssertES2016 = ContainsES2016,
    AssertES2015 = ContainsES2015,
    AssertGenerator = ContainsGenerator,

    // Scope Exclusions

Now I have all the pieces I need to give a thorough explanation of the base AST node structure in the TypeScript compiler. Let me compile this into a comprehensive explanation.

Here's the complete picture:

1. **`ReadonlyTextRange`** (line 33): The foundation - just `pos` and `end` (absolute offsets in the source text).

2. **`Node`** (line 942) extends `ReadonlyTextRange`:
   - `kind: SyntaxKind` - what syntactic construct it is (enum member like `FunctionDeclaration`, `Identifier`, etc.)
   - `flags: NodeFlags` - parse-time bit-flags that track various properties
   - `modifierFlagsCache: ModifierFlags` - cached modifier information like public/private/static
   - `transformFlags: TransformFlags` - tracks which transforms need to apply to this node and its subtree
   - `id?: NodeId` - an internal numeric identifier used to look up associated metadata
   - `parent: Node` - reference to the parent node, established during the binding phase
   - `original?: Node` - points to the original node if this one has been updated or transformed
   - `emitNode?: EmitNode` - metadata attached during emission, used by transformers

3. **`NodeFlags`** (line 782

) - parse-time flags that categorize different aspects of the node. Syntactic flags like `Let`, `Const`, and `Using` mark variable declaration types, while context flags like `YieldContext` and `AwaitContext` indicate the parsing context. There are also error tracking flags to mark nodes with errors, aggregation flags, synthesized flags for transformer-created nodes, and binder-set flags that track things like whether the node contains `this` or has explicit/implicit returns.

The `transformFlags` field stores information about what transformations this node and its subtree need, along with scope exclusion masks that prevent certain flags from propagating out of specific node types. There's also a `HasComputedFlags` bit that signals whether the flags have already been computed for the subtree.

`NodeLinks` is a separate structure that isn't stored directly on the node itself but is looked up separately. Several specialized node interfaces layer on top for specific purposes—`LocalsContainer` adds symbol tables for scope tracking, `FlowContainer` holds control flow information, `JSDocContainer` manages documentation, and `Declaration` nodes carry symbol references. The crucial design pattern here is that `NodeLinks` doesn't live on the node itself; instead, the checker maintains a parallel map keyed by node `id`, keeping the parse tree clean of checker state. Similarly, `emitNode` starts undefined and gets attached lazily by transformers only when needed.

Here is the complete picture of `Node`, assembled from `types.ts`.

---

## The base container: `Node` (types.ts:942)

```typescript
export interface Node extends ReadonlyTextRange {
    readonly kind: SyntaxKind;
    readonly flags: NodeFlags;
    /** @internal */ modifierFlagsCache: ModifierFlags;
    /** @internal */ readonly transformFlags: TransformFlags;
    /** @internal */ id?: NodeId;
    readonly parent: Node;
    /** @internal */ original?: Node;
    /** @internal */ emitNode?: EmitNode;
}
```

It extends `ReadonlyTextRange` (types.ts:33), which is just two absolute byte offsets into the source text:

```typescript
export interface ReadonlyTextRange {
    readonly pos: number;  // inclusive start
    readonly end: number;  // exclusive end
}
```

---

## The five groups of fields

### 1. What construct it is — `kind` (SyntaxKind)

`kind` is a member of the large `SyntaxKind` const-enum (types.ts:40). Every possible syntactic construct — `Identifier`, `FunctionDeclaration`, `BinaryExpression`, `SourceFile`, keywords, punctuation tokens — has exactly one value. All specialised node interfaces (`FunctionDeclaration`, `Identifier`, etc.) narrow `kind` to a literal so the union discriminates cleanly.

### 2. Parse-time bit-flags — `flags` (NodeFlags, types.ts:782)

`NodeFlags` encodes facts the *parser* observes and facts the *binder* annotates later. They live in three natural clusters:

**Syntactic facts** — recorded by the parser from grammar context:
- `Let` / `Const` / `Using` / `AwaitUsing` — which keyword introduced a variable declaration
- `OptionalChain` — node is part of a `?.` chain
- `Namespace` / `NestedNamespace` — module-declaration shape
- `Synthesized` — node was manufactured by a transformer, not parsed from real text
- `JSDoc` / `JsonFile` — parsing mode when the node was produced

**Parser context flags** — inherited from the parsing context stack, recorded on every node produced inside that context:
- `YieldContext`, `AwaitContext`, `DecoratorContext`, `DisallowInContext`, `DisallowConditionalTypesContext`, `JavaScriptFile`, `InWithStatement`, `Ambient`

These form the `ContextFlags` mask and are how the checker can ask "was this expression written inside an async function?" without re-walking ancestors.

**Error and aggregation bits** (parser and incremental):
- `ThisNodeHasError` — the parser hit a recovery point producing this node
- `ThisNodeOrAnySubNodesHasError` — propagated upward
- `HasAggregatedChildData` — a one-time write guard for per-subtree caching

**Binder-annotated flags** (set after parsing, never by the parser itself):
- `ExportContext`, `ContainsThis`, `HasImplicitReturn`, `HasExplicitReturn`, `HasAsyncFunctions`, `GlobalAugmentation`
- Internal: `TypeCached`, `Deprecated`, `Unreachable`, `PossiblyContainsDynamicImport`, `PossiblyContainsImportMeta`

### 3. Transform-readiness — `transformFlags` (TransformFlags, types.ts:8212)

`transformFlags` is a subtree-accumulating field that answers: *which transformation passes must touch this node or anything inside it?*

The lower bits are **fact flags** — what this node or any descendant contains:
- `ContainsTypeScript`, `ContainsJsx`, `ContainsESNext` down through `ContainsES2015`, `ContainsGenerator`, `ContainsDestructuringAssignment`, `ContainsYield`, `ContainsAwait`, `ContainsDecorators`, `ContainsClassFields`, etc.

The upper sentinel bit `HasComputedFlags` (bit 31) marks that the subtree roll-up has been done; the node factory sets it when it stamps the flags during construction so it isn't recomputed.

The enum also defines **scope-exclusion masks** (`FunctionExcludes`, `ArrowFunctionExcludes`, `ClassExcludes`, etc.). When a child node's flags are propagated up into a parent, bits listed in the parent's exclusion mask are stripped. This is how `ContainsYield` doesn't leak out of a function boundary: the parent function node's exclude mask clears it before the value reaches the grandparent.

### 4. Position in the tree — `parent` and `id`

`parent: Node` is filled in by the binder during its first pass. The parser produces parent-free nodes; the binder wires them into the tree in declaration order.

`id?: NodeId` (an opaque `number`) is the node's key into the checker's side-table. Rather than putting checker state directly on each node, the checker maintains a parallel array `nodeLinks: NodeLinks[]` indexed by `id`. The `id` is assigned lazily on first access — nodes that the checker never touches have no `id`.

### 5. Mutable slots attached by later passes

**`original?: Node`** — when a transformer replaces a node, the replacement carries a back-pointer to the node it replaced. This is how the printer and source-map emitter can find the original text range for a synthesised replacement.

**`emitNode?: EmitNode`** (types.ts:8312) — lazily attached by transformers. Contains everything the emitter needs that isn't structural syntax:
- `flags: EmitFlags` and `internalFlags: InternalEmitFlags` — whether to suppress source maps, force single-line output, mark as a helper name, etc.
- `leadingComments`, `trailingComments`, `commentRange` — synthesised comments and the range to use for real ones
- `sourceMapRange`, `tokenSourceMapRanges` — override the text range the source-map emitter uses
- `constantValue` — a folded literal value for const enum members
- `helpers` — emitter helpers (e.g. `__awaiter`) to inject near this node
- `autoGenerate` — how to generate the text of a synthesised identifier
- `classThis`, `assignedName` — class-specific emit bookkeeping

---

## What is intentionally *not* on `Node`

Three things that were once on `Node` were moved to narrower mixin interfaces (see the comment at types.ts:951, referencing the PR that split them):

| Field | Where it lives now |
|---|---|
| `symbol`, `localSymbol` | `Declaration` sub-interface |
| `locals`, `nextContainer` | `LocalsContainer` sub-interface (types.ts:967) |
| `flowNode` | `FlowContainer` sub-interface (types.ts:973) |

And `NodeLinks` (types.ts:6272) — the checker's mutable side-table entry — lives entirely off the node, reachable only through `node.id`. It holds `resolvedType`, `resolvedSignature`, `resolvedSymbol`, `NodeCheckFlags`, JSX-related caches, deferred-checking sets, and everything else the type checker accumulates. Keeping this off the node preserves the parse tree as a clean, mostly-immutable value the checker can share across incremental rebuilds.

---

## Summary map

```
Node
├── pos, end                   ← byte offsets in source text (from ReadonlyTextRange)
├── kind: SyntaxKind           ← which syntactic construct
├── flags: NodeFlags           ← parse-time + binder facts (30 bits)
├── modifierFlagsCache         ← computed modifier bits, cached here to avoid re-walking
├── transformFlags             ← subtree transform needs (propagated upward, internal)
├── parent                     ← set by binder; null until then
├── id                         ← key into checker's NodeLinks side-table (lazy)
├── original                   ← back-pointer to pre-transform node (transform phase)
└── emitNode                   ← lazy bag of emit/source-map metadata (transform phase)
```
